channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Concerns raised over AUKUS Pillar II undersea drones and lack of implementation detail
item.description: The article examines Australia’s evolving AUKUS defence arrangement, focusing on the emerging role of uncrewed undersea systems under Pillar II of the agreement. While undersea drones are broadly seen...

item.title: Kano NDC Candidate Criticizes Gov Yusuf Over Rising Insecurity in the State
item.description: The governorship candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) in Kano State, former Deputy Governor Aminu Abdussalam Gwarzo, don sharply criticize Governor Abba Kabir Yusuf over the worsening se...

item.title: Curated alternative news roundup on nuclear issues, climate, and global politics
item.description: This article is a curated roundup of alternative news items focused on nuclear issues, climate change, geopolitics, and related social and environmental topics. It presents a broad selection of headli...

item.title: Melbourne woman appears in court on terrorism charges after return from Syria
item.description: A Melbourne woman accused of terrorism offences has appeared in court for the first time following her return to Australia from Syria in 2025. The woman, widely described in media reports as an “ISIS ...

item.title: IHC adjourns hearing on Imaan Mazari and Hadi Chattha sentence suspension despite SC directive deadline
item.description: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) on Monday adjourned the hearing of sentence suspension petitions filed by human rights lawyers Imaan Zainab Mazari-Hazir and Hadi Ali Chattha in the controversial social...

item.title: PNP reports significant drop in focus crimes nationwide in May 2026 amid intensified operations
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) reported a significant decrease in focus crime incidents across the country in May 2026, marking a 25% drop compared to the same period last year. According to PNP...

item.title: Senegal Captain Assane Sarr Leads Team to U-17 AFCON Final Against Tanzania
item.description: Senegal's U-17 team is now one match away from winning the TotalEnergies CAF Under-17 Africa Cup of Nations in Morocco 2026, with their 16-year-old captain and goalkeeper Assane Sarr playing a key rol...

item.title: ‘Sema na Waziri’ programme handles 163 public complaints since launch, ministry reports progress
item.description: The Tanzanian government’s citizen engagement initiative known as ‘Sema na Waziri’ has recorded 163 public grievances since it was launched in March, according to the Ministry of Constitution and Lega...

item.title: Gauteng Restricts Provincial Funding to South African Social Workers Only
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Social Development (GDSD) has introduced new funding rules preventing non-profit organisations from using provincial grants to pay non-South African social workers. According...

item.title: Paris celebrates football victory as authorities address post-match riot concerns
item.description: Paris was filled with celebration following a major football triumph, widely linked to a high-profile European club success, as fans gathered across the city to mark the historic win. However, the joy...

item.title: Imran Khan Has Not Met Former Army Chief in Adiala Jail, Says Sister Aleema
item.description: Aleema Khan, sister of PTI founder Imran Khan, has strongly denied rumours circulating on social media regarding a meeting between Imran Khan and a former army chief at Adiala jail. In a post on X, sh...

item.title: President Ruto Hosts First-Ever Madaraka Day Celebrations in Wajir
item.description: President William Ruto presided over the 2026 Madaraka Day celebrations at the newly built Wajir Stadium, marking a historic first for the North Eastern region of Kenya. The event, held on 1st June 20...

item.title: US and Iran exchange limited strikes amid fragile ceasefire and ongoing negotiations
item.description: Despite an existing ceasefire, the United States and Iran carried out fresh military strikes over the weekend, indicating continued tensions in West Asia. The US Central Command stated that it conduct...

item.title: Africa Accelerates Shift Toward Solar, Wind, and Distributed Energy Systems
item.description: Africa is rapidly expanding its renewable energy capacity as governments and investors prioritize solar, wind, and battery storage over traditional energy sources such as coal and large hydropower. Th...

item.title: WFP Expands Efforts to Address Food Security and Nutrition Gaps in Tanzania
item.description: The World Food Programme (WFP) has stepped up its commitment to support Tanzania in addressing ongoing food security and nutrition challenges affecting millions of people. Despite steady economic grow...

item.title: CBC to restructure over 700 government training institutions under UNNATI initiative
item.description: The Capacity Building Commission (CBC) is currently leading a major structural transformation of more than 700 training institutions across India that are responsible for training government employees...

item.title: New Virtual Museum Offers Access to Hundreds of Historic and Obsolete Operating Systems
item.description: A new digital preservation project known as the Virtual OS Museum allows users to explore and run around 570 historic and obsolete operating systems. The initiative has gained attention after reports ...

item.title: Jason Feger leads flag-to-flag to win $5,000 MARS Late Model feature at Red Hill Speedway
item.description: Jason Feger delivered a dominant performance on Saturday night, leading every lap to capture the MARS Late Model Championship Series feature at Red Hill Speedway in Sumner, Illinois. The 30-lap main e...

item.title: DA inspects Manila rice vendor over alleged alteration of P50 price cap signage
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) conducted an inspection in Manila to monitor compliance with the government’s P50 per kilo rice price cap initiative. During the inspection, authorities reportedly d...

item.title: Oyo school abduction triggers teachers’ strike as federal government deploys rescue team
item.description: Teachers in Oyo State under the Nigeria Union of Teachers (NUT) have begun an indefinite strike following the continued abduction of teachers and pupils from schools in Oriire Local Government Area. T...
